summary: Well-scoped plan with minimal blast radius. The new palette.ts module cleanly encapsulates geo classification and palette family logic. The geoToPalette function signature is preserved, so the VisualParams interface, evolution.ts, and particleField.ts require zero changes. Downstream consumers already operate on numeric hue/saturation values and are unaffected.
strengths: Function signature of geoToPalette preserved — zero downstream breakage
VisualParams interface unchanged — evolution and rendering layers untouched
New palette.ts module follows existing module split conventions (single responsibility)
Fallback for unknown geo is explicit and tested, maintaining graceful degradation
Determinism guarantee maintained via seed mixing within family range
Privacy model intact — classification table is internal, output remains numeric only

concerns.description: Test T-008-19 checks JSON.stringify(result) does not contain 'US' or 'CA'. These are 2-char strings that cannot appear as substrings of JSON-serialized numbers, so this is safe. However, if palette family IDs (e.g. 'tropical') were ever accidentally included in VisualParams, the test would not catch them. Consider adding family ID strings to the privacy assertion.
concerns.recommendation: In Step 3, extend T-008-19 to also assert that no geo class strings ('tropical', 'northern', etc.) appear in serialized output.

concerns.description: Evolution drift of ±15-30° can push hue outside a narrow palette family range (e.g. a family with hueRange=25). This is acknowledged in the plan's risks section and is acceptable artistically, but could reduce the legibility of the geo-to-palette mapping over time.
concerns.recommendation: Document this as intentional. No code change needed — the organic drift is a feature.

concerns.description: The geo classification table (~20-30 country codes) will leave many visitors in the 'unknown' fallback. This is a content completeness issue, not an architectural one, and can be expanded later without any structural changes.
concerns.recommendation: Ship with the initial table; expand coverage in a follow-up if telemetry-free observation suggests too many fallbacks.

dependency_check.US-005: sessionSeed module — required for seed mixing. Already implemented.
dependency_check.US-008: mappings.ts with geoToPalette — the function being upgraded. Already implemented.
dependency_check.US-009: evolution.ts — operates on numeric VisualParams output. Already implemented and unaffected by this change.

files_impact.src/visual/palette.ts: CREATE — geo classification table + palette family definitions (~80-100 lines)
files_impact.src/visual/mappings.ts: MODIFY — replace geoToPalette body, add import from palette.ts (~15 lines changed)
files_impact.tests/visual/palette.test.ts: CREATE — unit tests for classification and family logic (~60-80 lines)
files_impact.tests/visual/mappings.test.ts: MODIFY — update/add palette-family-aware test cases (~30-40 lines changed)
files_impact.src/visual/evolution.ts: UNCHANGED — operates on numeric paletteHue, no coupling to palette families
files_impact.src/visual/systems/particleField.ts: UNCHANGED — consumes paletteHue and paletteSaturation as numbers
